name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart Columbus, Ohio — CheckSmart is an Ohio-based financial services store offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay, and ref...
description_long:
CheckSmart is a brick-and-mortar financial services retailer with multiple locations across Ohio, including several in the Columbus metro area. The company operates physical storefronts focused on serving consumers who need in-person access to a range of everyday financial services. The Columbus location on 2496 Morse Rd sits at the northeast corner of Morse Rd and Cleveland Ave and is one of at least five Columbus-area CheckSmart branches.

CheckSmart's core in-store offerings center on transactional financial services: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment. The stores also sell Green Dot Visa Debit Cards and exchange unused gift cards for cash — services aimed at underbanked consumers who need accessible, immediate financial tools without a traditional bank account. Customers can schedule appointments online before visiting to streamline the in-store process.

On the lending side, CheckSmart does not originate loans directly. Instead, it provides Ohio residents with access to loan products from an unaffiliated third-party lender. Customers apply in-store with required documents — government-issued ID, proof of income, proof of address, and contact information — and lending decisions are rendered quickly. CheckSmart positions itself as a facilitator and customer service hub, not the actual creditor.

For consumers who need check cashing or basic financial transaction services, CheckSmart is a practical option with broad Ohio coverage and convenient weekday hours. However, because loans are issued by an unnamed third-party lender, borrowers have limited transparency about rates and terms upfront. The in-store-only loan process and restricted weekend hours may be inconvenient for some. CheckSmart is best suited to underbanked Ohio residents who need in-person financial services, not consumers seeking competitive online lending rates.
pros: Multiple Columbus-area locations plus coverage across Ohio (Heath, Marion, Marysville, Reynoldsburg, Springfield, Whitehall)
Extended Friday hours (8:00 AM – 8:00 PM) compared to typical weekday schedule
Green Dot Visa Debit Card available in-store for unbanked customers
Gift card exchange for cash — converts idle gift cards to spendable funds
Online appointment scheduling to reduce in-store wait time
Broad transaction services under one roof: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay
Quick lending decisions for third-party loan referrals with in-person application support
cons: CheckSmart is not the actual lender — loans come from an unnamed unaffiliated third party, obscuring rate and term transparency
Loan applications are in-store only; no fully online loan process available
Limited weekend availability: Saturday closes at 4:00 PM, Sunday at 5:00 PM
Ohio-only footprint limits accessibility for out-of-state consumers
No disclosed APR ranges or loan amounts on the website — requires an in-store visit to get details
best_for: Underbanked Ohio residents who need check cashing or money orders without a bank account
Consumers who prefer in-person financial service assistance over online applications
Columbus-area residents needing gift card liquidation or Green Dot debit card access
Short-term borrowers comfortable with third-party loan referrals and in-store processing
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best for underbanked Ohio residents who need reliable in-person access to check cashing, money orders, and everyday financial transactions. The main caveat on the lending side is that CheckSmart is a referral intermediary, not a direct lender — borrowers should expect to ask specifically about the third-party lender's rates and terms before committing, as none are disclosed online.
services: Green Dot Visa Debit Card (in-store purchase)
In-person customer service and financial guidance
